records.ety: [L. scrupulus a small sharp or pointed stone, the twenty-fourth part of an ounce, a scruple, uneasiness, doubt, dim. of scrupus a rough or sharp stone, anxiety, uneasiness; perh. akin to Gr. <?/ the chippings of stone, <?/ a razor, Skr. kshura: cf. F. scrupule.]

defs: A weight of twenty grains; the third part of a dram.
Hence, a very small quantity; a particle.
quotes: I will not bate thee a scruple.

defs: Hesitation as to action from the difficulty of determining what is right or expedient; unwillingness, doubt, or hesitation proceeding from motives of conscience.
quotes: He was made miserable by the conflict between his tastes and his scruples.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Scrupled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Scrupling (?).]

defs: To be reluctant or to hesitate, as regards an action, on account of considerations of conscience or expedience.
quotes: We are often over-precise, scrupling to say or do those things which lawfully we may.
Men scruple at the lawfulness of a set form of divine worship.

defs: To regard with suspicion; to hesitate at; to question.
quotes: Others long before them . . . scrupled more the books of heretics than of gentiles.

defs: To excite scruples in; to cause to scruple.
quotes: Letters which did still scruple many of them.
